examples: <sqf>myUnit setDir 45; // will set myUnit to face North-East</sqf>
<sqf>myUnit setDir -675; // will also set myUnit to face North-East (= 45-360-360)</sqf>

<sqf>
MyUnit setDir 30;
MyUnit setFormDir 30; // needed for AI to keep the given direction
</sqf>

<sqf>
// provided _myMine is local
_myMine setDir 45;
_myMine setPosWorld getPosWorld _myMine;

// or
[_myMine, 45] remoteExec ["setDir"];
</sqf>

<sqf>
// sets the direction of an object relative to a parent object's direction
_child setDir (_parentDir + (_childDir - _parentDir));
</sqf>
name: setDir
problem_notes: * The effect is {{Icon|localEffect|32}} when using [[setDir]] on a mine. Use a position modification to broadcast [[setDir]]'s change (see {{Link|#Example 4}})
setting direction ''after'' position may lead to weird behaviours - see {{Link|#Notes}}
